captions.text: Whereas RAG gives you one way to

give additional information
to a large language model,

there's another technique
called fine-tuning,

which is another way to
give it more information.

In particular, if you have
context that is bigger,

that can fit into
the input length or

the context window
length of the LLM,

then fine-tuning
gives you another way

to get LLM to absorb
this information.

Fine-tuning also turns
out to be useful for

getting the LLM output text
in a certain given style.

is a bit harder than RAG.

called pre-training.

Now, let's say I want to

optimistic attitude

There's a technique called

Now, maybe shifting

isn't that helpful
an application,

many real applications.

One class of applications
that fine-tuning is useful

is when the task isn't easy
to define in a prompt.
For example, if you want to use
